facts: The British government taxed the American colonists after the French and Indian War to pay war debts, but colonists had no representatives in Parliament to vote on those taxes, which led to the slogan 'no taxation without representation'.
George Washington served as the commander of the Continental Army during the Revolutionary War, not as a political leader like Thomas Jefferson, who wrote the Declaration of Independence.
The Declaration of Independence, written mainly by Thomas Jefferson, announced in 1776 that the American colonies were free and independent from Britain, not just protesting unfair taxes.
Benjamin Franklin helped the American cause by securing military support from France, which was a crucial ally because France was a rival of Britain.
The American Revolution lasted from 1775 to 1783, and the colonists won their independence after defeating the British army with help from France, not just by fighting alone.
misconceptions: The American Revolution began because colonists simply refused to pay any taxes at all.
George Washington wrote the Declaration of Independence.
